value_units.nominal_example: 60,592 means 60,592 million dollars, or about $60.6 billion.
value_units.real_toggle: Optional latest-quarter-dollar restatement using the GDP implicit price deflator (GDPDEF).
value_units.percent_of_gdp_toggle: Optional percent-of-GDP view. Annualizes the quarterly TDC flow (in millions) and divides by nominal GDP (SAAR, billions): percent = value_millions * 4 / (nominal_gdp_saar_bil * 1000) * 100.

site.title: TDCest
site.tagline: Treasury-attributed component of deposits: estimator ladder, receipt boundaries, fiscal shell, and monetary cross-checks.
site.thesis: TDC should either add to deposits one-for-one or offset another deposit component one-for-one; this site compares the transaction ladder, fiscal-flow estimates, and monetary cross-checks to show where that identity is strongest and where the remaining boundaries still sit.

rows.summary_note: Table 5.1 bank-minor bridge is now usable as a historical default view inside the age-eligible window.

Current bank bridge remains the leading candidate, but the latest share is too stale under the current rule (stale-share years = 4). No fresher official public IRS bank-minor share path beyond TY2022 has been identified. Stop gate: historical_default_only_current_nondefault.

Best current public recurring ROW pilot. The main bridge is now MRV-first, while secondary visa lines stay outside the main ROW delta until payer identity and cash timing are stronger. Latest secondary visa sensitivity: 16.845 million. Combined Statement now confirms the broader CBSP main-account family, but not the exact MRV sub-account. Promotion checklist: 3 complete, 1 partial default blocker, 2 missing required checks. Stop gate: stop_at_mrv_nondefault_pilot. Stronger bounded FAH/FAM and OIG cash-route evidence is now loaded, but no public quarterly MRV cash series or global legal-remitter proof has been found.
Secondary recurring State-visa branch. Kept visible for scale and auditability, but it does not enter the main recurring ROW delta and is not in line for default promotion.
Bank interest corrections are live and bank noninterest outlay corrections are already inside Tier 3 narrow / partial. The missing piece is bank receipts, not bank transfer-side construction.

ROW interest corrections are live and narrow ROW noninterest outlay corrections are already inside Tier 3 narrow / partial. The missing piece is a defensible ROW receipt correction, not ROW transfer-side coverage.

Current bank bridge remains the leading candidate, but the latest share is too stale under the current rule (stale-share years = 4). No fresher official public IRS bank-minor share path beyond TY2022 has been identified. Stop gate: historical_default_only_current_nondefault.

Best current public recurring ROW pilot. The main bridge is now MRV-first, while secondary visa lines stay outside the main ROW delta until payer identity and cash timing are stronger. Latest secondary visa sensitivity: 16.845 million. Combined Statement now confirms the broader CBSP main-account family, but not the exact MRV sub-account. Promotion checklist: 3 complete, 1 partial default blocker, 2 missing required checks. Stop gate: stop_at_mrv_nondefault_pilot. Stronger bounded FAH/FAM and OIG cash-route evidence is now loaded, but no public quarterly MRV cash series or global legal-remitter proof has been found.
The fiscal-flow version exists as a real reconciliation shell around the ladder, not as a fully solved replacement estimator. Its main limitation is still unresolved receipt-side cells.

The monetary-disaggregated version is already strong as a diagnostic cross-check. The site prefers the depository target as the main cross-check and keeps the commercial-bank target as a stress view, not a headline estimator.
Table 5.1 bank-minor bridge is now usable as a historical default view inside the age-eligible window.
rows.next_finite_push: Integrate the historical bank window into polished Tier 3 research tables and figures.

rows.summary_note: Best current public recurring ROW pilot. The main bridge is now MRV-first, while secondary visa lines stay outside the main ROW delta until payer identity and cash timing are stronger. Latest secondary visa sensitivity: 16.845 million. Combined Statement now confirms the broader CBSP main-account family, but not the exact MRV sub-account. Promotion checklist: 3 complete, 1 partial default blocker, 2 missing required checks. Stop gate: stop_at_mrv_nondefault_pilot. Stronger bounded FAH/FAM and OIG cash-route evidence is now loaded, but no public quarterly MRV cash series or global legal-remitter proof has been found.
rows.next_finite_push: Tighten the MRV payment-chain and reconciliation layers around the stronger FAH/FAM/OIG bounded evidence dataset.

rows.summary_note: The site now has enough structured boundaries to package a cleaner end-state research view without solving every live blocker first.
rows.next_finite_push: Build the polished Tier 2 vs Tier 3, historical bank, and receipt-boundary figures and tables.

rows.summary_note: Current bank bridge remains the leading candidate, but the latest share is too stale under the current rule (stale-share years = 4). No fresher official public IRS bank-minor share path beyond TY2022 has been identified. Stop gate: historical_default_only_current_nondefault.
rows.next_finite_push: Keep the current window explicit and bounded; only revisit if a genuinely newer public IRS bank-minor share appears.

rows.summary_note: Fiscal shell is already live; keep using it as a reconciliation view rather than trying to solve weak receipt cells prematurely.
rows.next_finite_push: Keep the shell coherent and source-graded while folding in improved historical receipt views.

rows.summary_note: These branches are now useful mainly as exclusion accounting, not as serious promotion candidates.
rows.next_finite_push: No new hunt; keep these families visible and excluded.

rows.summary_note: These lines remain useful for sensitivity and exclusion logic, but they are low-payoff for the end-state estimator.
rows.next_finite_push: No active build beyond keeping OCC/FDIC/OFR lines explicitly bounded.

rows.summary_note: The site already has the monetary answer it needs: depository target as cross-check, commercial-bank target as stress case only.
rows.next_finite_push: Keep the depository target as the main cross-check and stop expanding the bank-target branch unless a new source family appears.

rows.interpretation: Latest live comparison between Tier 2 and default Tier 3, with current receipt boundaries still binding.
Latest historical age-eligible bank window showing how the nonzero historical bank receipt view changes Tier 3.

rows.notes: Bank corporate-tax receipt bridge using MTS quarterly cash totals and IRS Publication 16 Table 5.1 strict-depository shares.
Primary bank corporate-tax default-candidate bridge using MTS quarterly cash totals and IRS Publication 16 Table 5.1 depository-plus-BHC shares.
Upper benchmark only: MTS quarterly cash totals with broad finance-sector annual shares retained for scale comparison.
BEA/NIPA ROW current-receipts benchmark. Official macro benchmark, not a Treasury cash-payer default.
Proxy Treasury coupon-interest correction to the default bank perimeter.
Exact SOMA-based Treasury coupon-interest correction to the Fed.
Proxy Treasury coupon-interest correction to the rest of the world, with scale audited against BEA/FRED.
Positive-only Fed remittance convention preserved from the base estimator.
Mint or central-bank cash-factor adjustment used in the current Tier 3 build.
Current default bank nonborrow-receipt correction. This remains zero in the live source-backed build.
Current default ROW nonborrow-receipt correction. This remains zero in the live source-backed build.
Current default bank noninterest-outlay correction from MTS Financial Agent Services-style lines.
Current default narrow ROW noninterest-outlay correction from selected MTS foreign and international lines.
Treasury operating cash contribution carried with the existing negative cash-offset convention.
Default bank-perimeter Treasury-security transactions: U.S.-chartered banks, foreign banking offices in the U.S., and banks in U.S.-affiliated areas.
Fed/SOMA Treasury-security transactions in the reserve-user-flow ladder.
Rest-of-world Treasury-security transactions included in the bank-only headline.
OCC due-date timing sensitivity built from the annual bank non-tax pilot and current semiannual assessment cadence.
Revenue Collections Bank channel upper bound. Routing-heavy and excluded from the default counterparty map.
Revenue Collections Bank non-tax upper bound. Still routing-heavy and excluded from the default counterparty map.
ROW State/visa timing sensitivity built from the strict annual pilot and monthly NIV/IV issuance shares.
Broad security-heavy ROW outlay profile retained as a published sensitivity rather than the default narrow profile.
Natural-person credit-union Treasury-security transactions for the broad-depository sensitivity.

rows.review_rationale: The depository target now behaves materially better under the expanded Stage 1 block, while the commercial-bank-deposit target remains mostly unresolved and still carries a material bank-target-specific wedge.

rows.interpretation: The live Tier 3 bank receipt cell is still a zero default, not because bank receipts are irrelevant, but because current-quarter promotion remains blocked by stale public shares.
The live Tier 3 ROW receipt cell is still a zero default because the MRV branch remains a bounded bounded pilot rather than a promotable additive correction.
This is the best official public bank receipt bridge in the site, but the latest loaded bridge date can extend beyond the admissible historical overlay window.
This stricter depository-only bridge is useful for uncertainty bounds, but its latest loaded bridge date can also sit beyond the admissible historical overlay window.
This upper benchmark remains useful for scale checking, but it is not admissible as a bank default receipt correction.
This overlay is the site’s main historical receipt-side improvement path for bank analysis.
This lower bound keeps the historical bank overlay from being treated as a single-point truth by showing the conservative additive effect versus default Tier 3.
USDO collection accounts, sweep mechanics, deposit notifications, OF-158 support, and GFSC reconciliation are now publicly evidenced.
BEA ROW receipts help contextualize scale, but they do not solve the Treasury cash-payer identity problem.

rows.plain_english_summary: The most direct theory statement: TDC is the Treasury-driven part of deposit change seen from the domestic nonbank deposit-user side.
rows.measurement_note: The site does not directly observe every DU-side term. The live ladder approximates this identity from Treasury-security transactions, Treasury operating cash, remittances, and bounded fiscal corrections.
rows.limit_note: This is the conceptual accounting identity. Current estimates are narrower measurement approximations, not a full direct DU ledger.

rows.latex: \Delta D^{TDC}_{DU} = (Q^T_{DU\to RU}-Q^T_{RU\to DU}) + (I^T + R^T_{RU} + \Pi^F_T - G^{ND}_{RU} - DS^T_{RU}) - \Delta TOC
rows.plain_english_summary: The main Treasury-cash identity: TDC depends on Treasury-security settlement with reserve-side counterparties, Treasury-related cash flows with those counterparties, and the change in Treasury operating cash.
rows.measurement_note: This is the closest theory frame for the current live estimator ladder. The site explicitly uses Treasury operating cash (TOC), Treasury-security transaction terms, and positive Fed remittances, then layers in narrower fiscal-flow corrections.
rows.limit_note: The site does not yet directly measure every RU-side issuance, receipt, outlay, and debt-service term in this exact symbolic form.

rows.display_title: Fed Remittances and Deferred Asset Treatment
rows.plain_english_summary: Negative Fed earnings do not imply Treasury pays the Fed. They build a deferred asset and suppress future remittances until that deferred asset is worked off.
rows.measurement_note: The site already reflects this by clipping Fed remittances at zero before quarter aggregation and treating negative H.4.1 values as deferred-asset accounting rather than negative Treasury cash flow.
rows.limit_note: The live measurement uses the public remittance series as a practical proxy rather than reconstructing the full Fed earnings identity term by term.

rows.latex: \Delta D^{TDC}_{DU} = (\Delta M-\Delta C-\Delta X) - (\Delta L^B_{DU}+\Delta A^{B,NT}_{DU}) - \Delta A^{CB,NT}_{DU} - \Delta F^{NT}_{DU} - \varepsilon
rows.plain_english_summary: A residual version of TDC: start from total deposit change, subtract major non-Treasury drivers, and treat the remainder as the Treasury contribution.
rows.measurement_note: This maps to the site’s monetary/disaggregated branch and decomposition proxies, which are used as diagnostics and cross-checks rather than as the headline estimator.
rows.limit_note: The site does not treat this residual approach as a headline estimate because large perimeter and measurement wedges still remain.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{mkt,bank}_{TDC,t} = \Delta TS^{tx}_{Fed,t} + \Delta TS^{tx}_{Banks,t} + \Delta TS^{tx}_{ROW,t} - \Delta TOC^{tx}_{Treasury,t} + Remit^{+}_{Fed,t}
rows.plain_english_summary: The simplest live estimate: Treasury-security transactions plus positive Fed remittances minus Treasury operating cash.
rows.measurement_note: This is the site’s live bank-only base headline and the main implemented approximation to the Treasury-cash-constraint identity in the transaction-data era.
rows.limit_note: It is still a measurement approximation. It is bank-only, marketable-Treasury-focused, and not a full direct implementation of the theory identities.

rows.plain_english_summary: The bank-only headline after removing coupon-interest distortions from Fed, banks, and ROW Treasury holdings.
rows.measurement_note: This is the site’s strongest transfer-side cleanup layer and one of the most informative live comparisons for downstream work.
rows.limit_note: Coupon terms are still proxies built from public holdings and maturity inputs, not observed cash-settlement flows.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{Tier3,bank}_{TDC,t} = \widehat{\Delta D}^{Tier2,bank}_{TDC,t} - Outlay^{Banks}_t - Outlay^{ROW}_t + Receipt^{Banks}_t + Receipt^{ROW}_t + CashFactor_t
rows.plain_english_summary: Tier 2 plus narrow fiscal-flow corrections for bank and ROW outlays, bank and ROW receipts, and mint or central-bank cash factors.
rows.measurement_note: This is the best live corrected estimate in the site. It is the main fiscal-flow-corrected ladder output used for current-quarter work.
rows.limit_note: Receipt-side cells remain partial. Current bank receipts stay bounded, and MRV remains a bounded bounded ROW pilot.

rows.plain_english_summary: A historical-only Tier 3 variant that adds the age-eligible IRS Table 5.1 bank receipt correction.
rows.measurement_note: This is the site’s strongest bank receipt result. It shows how Tier 3 changes when the bank receipt bridge is allowed inside the historical age-eligible window.
rows.limit_note: The current-quarter bridge remains blocked by stale public IRS bank-minor shares, so this cannot be promoted into the live default estimate.

rows.plain_english_summary: A bounded rest-of-world receipt sensitivity using the MRV / CBSP recurring fee branch.
rows.measurement_note: This is the only serious recurring ROW receipt branch currently kept visible. It is kept separate from the live default estimate and used as a boundary marker.
rows.limit_note: It remains blocked by legal-remitter or debited-account proof and by the lack of public quarterly MRV cash timing.

rows.latex: \widehat{\Delta D}^{decomp}_{TDC,t} \approx \Delta M_t - \Delta C_t - \Delta X_t - \text{non-Treasury deposit drivers}
rows.plain_english_summary: A diagnostic residual framework used to see whether the ladder is directionally plausible against broader deposit targets.
rows.measurement_note: The site uses the depository target as the main monetary cross-check and the commercial-bank target as a stress case.
rows.limit_note: This branch is intentionally not treated as the headline estimator because the unresolved wedge is still too large and too perimeter-dependent.
